sections.paragraph:
ἐνερείδω (Hom. et al.; grave ins Sb 5909) to cause to enter by exerting force, thrust in pf. pass. ἐνήρεισμαι be thrust in, become fixed (cp. Plut., Mor. 327b; 344c) ἔρις ἐνήρεισται (gener. accepted conjecture of Zahn for the impossible ἐνείρισται of the mss.) ἐν ὑμῖν is firmly rooted among you IEph 8:1.—DELG s.v. ἐρείδω.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 336
